title: Poriyal

ingredients.title: Vegetables
items: 2 cups finely chopped green beans (or carrot, cabbage, or beans & carrot mix)
2 tbsp grated coconut (fresh or frozen, unsweetened)

ingredients.title: For Tempering
items: 2 tsp oil (preferably coconut oil for authentic flavor)
2 dried red chilies, broken into halves

instruction.title: 1. Blanch the Vegetables
steps: Bring a pot of water to boil. Add a pinch of salt and turmeric.
Blanch the chopped beans and carrot for 2–3 minutes, until tender-crisp.
Drain immediately and immerse in ice-cold water to retain color and crunch. Drain again and set aside.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Tempering
steps: Heat coconut oil in a wide pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds; let them splutter.
Add urad dal and chana dal; fry till golden brown.
Add dried red chilies, green chilies, curry leaves, and hing. Sauté briefly to release aromas.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Vegetables
steps: Add the blanched vegetables to the pan.
Toss gently on medium heat for 3–4 minutes to mix flavors, but avoid overcooking. The vegetables should remain slightly crisp.

instruction.title: 4. Finish with Coconut
steps: Add grated coconut and mix gently.
Optional: drizzle a few drops of coconut oil for extra flavor and gloss.

instruction.title: 5. Plating & Presentation
steps: Serve immediately, garnished with extra grated coconut if desired.
Works beautifully as a side with steaming hot rice, sambar, or rasam.

chefTips: Texture matters: Don’t overcook vegetables; they should remain crisp-tender.
Tempering oil: Use coconut oil for authentic South Indian flavor; olive or vegetable oil will taste different.
Coconut: Fresh is best; if using frozen, thaw completely and squeeze excess water.
Visual appeal: Mix vegetables of different colors for a vibrant, restaurant-worthy look.
